DGTS, Mumbai Zonal Unit Participates in GST Samvaad Roundtable in Mumbai
articles.full_content:
DGTS Engages with MSME Stakeholders at GST Samvaad Roundtable in Mumbai
:Mumbai, April 17, 2026
The Directorate General of Taxpayer Services (DGTS), Mumbai Zonal Unit (MZU), participated in the GST Samvaad – Roundtable Consultation held in Mumbai on April 17, 2026. The event provided a platform for interaction between GST authorities and stakeholders, particularly from the MSME sector to deliberate on issues related to GST compliance, policy developments and facilitation measures.
Shri Sumit Kumar, Principal Additional Director General, DGTS, MZU along with officers of the unit actively engaged with stakeholders during the roundtable. Their participation reflects DGTS’s continued commitment to strengthening taxpayer outreach and fostering constructive dialogue with industry.
The consultation was organised by India SME Forum, a leading organisation representing MSMEs across the country. The initiative aims to bridge the gap between taxpayers and policymakers by facilitating discussions on practical challenges and policy interventions under the GST framework.
Key discussions during the session focused on recent GST developments, including compliance-related challenges, procedural simplifications, and facilitation measures for MSMEs, especially those operating in digital, e-commerce, and multi-state environments. Participants also deliberated on emerging areas such as the GST Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT), rate rationalisation, refund mechanisms, registration processes and technological systems like the Invoice Management System (IMS).
DGTS officers shared insights on taxpayer services, grievance redressal, and the importance of awareness initiatives in improving compliance. Shri Sumit Kumar addressed queries and concerns raised by participants and encouraged collaboration with trade bodies for organising taxpayer education programmes. He also highlighted an upcoming outreach programme scheduled for April 24, 2026, in collaboration with CII, Western Region, on the Duty Deferment Facility and AEO Scheme under Customs.
The roundtable concluded with a shared emphasis on simplifying procedures, reducing compliance burden, and improving ease of doing business for MSMEs. The feedback received is expected to contribute to strengthening the GST ecosystem.
DGTS, under CBIC, plays a key role in taxpayer awareness and grievance redressal. During FY 2025–26, DGTS, MZU conducted 62 outreach programmes. Recent initiatives include sessions on GST awareness, grievance redressal, and sector-specific education.
PIB Mumbai Sriyanka Chatterjee/Robin Singh/Parshuram Kor

Review Meeting of Cross-Functional Team on Labour Codes held at Special State Office, EPFO Gangtok
articles.full_content:
A review meeting of the Cross-Functional Team (CFT) under the Ministry of Labour & Employment was held at the Special State Office, EPFO, Gangtok to review progress on implementation of the new Labour Codes and strengthen inter-departmental coordination in Sikkim.
The meeting was chaired by Shri Kesang N. Tenzing, Regional Provident Fund Commissioner, and attended by Shri Nirmal Choudhary representing MSME, Shri Pawan Kumar Sharma from ESIC and Shri Mainak from PIB, along with other officials. The officers deliberated extensively on the transition roadmap for the Code on Wages (2019), Industrial Relations Code (2020), Social Security Code (2020) and the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (2020), focusing on awareness generation, compliance facilitation and stakeholder engagement across the state.
During the discussions, emphasis was laid on intensifying Information, Education and Communication (IEC) activities so that employers and employees are well informed about the provisions of the Labour Codes, including social security coverage, wage protection and workplace safety standards. Plans were also reviewed for joint outreach programmes in collaboration with industry bodies and trade associations to ensure a smooth transition to the new legal framework.
Shri Pawan Kumar Sharma from ESIC highlighted the encouraging outcomes of the recent SPREE campaign conducted in Sikkim and shared experiences on expanding healthcare coverage and onboarding new establishments. It was also informed that the list of establishments identified under the Employees’ Enrolment Campaign — launched on 1st November and open till 30th April — would be shared among departments to facilitate coordinated enrolment and awareness efforts.
Shri Mainak from PIB outlined communication strategies to enhance public outreach, including planned media coverage and dissemination of simplified information regarding the Labour Codes to improve public understanding and participation.
The meeting concluded with a resolve to organise regular review interactions and collaborative awareness drives across Sikkim, ensuring that the objectives of labour reforms and expanded social security reach are effectively realised in the state.

Ministry of Mines Organises Roadshow on Critical Mineral Blocks and Exploration Licence Blocks in Hyderabad
articles.full_content:
The Ministry of Mines, Government of India, is organising a roadshow on the 7th Tranche of Auction of Critical and Strategic Mineral Blocks and the 2nd Tranche of Exploration Licence (EL) Blocks on 13th April 2026 at Hyderabad International Convention Centre (HICC), Gachibowli, Hyderabad.
The roadshow is being graced by the Union Minister for Coal and Mines, Shri G. Kishan Reddy, along with senior officials of the Ministry of Mines, representatives from State Governments, industry stakeholders, and prospective bidders.
The 7th tranche comprises 19 critical and strategic mineral blocks, including minerals such as tungsten, lithium, tin and rock phosphate, which are vital for sectors like clean energy, advanced technologies and fertilisers. With six tranches already conducted, 46 critical mineral blocks have been successfully auctioned so far, reflecting the Government’s continued efforts to strengthen mineral security and reduce import dependence.
The event also highlights the 2nd Tranche of Exploration Licence (EL) Blocks, aimed at enhancing private sector participation in exploration, particularly for deep-seated and critical minerals. The Exploration Licence regime provides a structured framework for reconnaissance and prospecting, thereby strengthening the exploration ecosystem in the country.
The roadshow includes detailed presentations on mineral potential, auction framework, policy reforms. An interactive session with stakeholders is also being held to address queries and facilitate wider participation.
This initiative is aligned with the Government’s vision of building a robust and self-reliant mineral sector, supporting the clean energy transition, and advancing the goals of Atmanirbhar Bharat.

PIB Itanagar Organises ‘Varta’ on Role of Media in the Age of Artificial Intelligence at Pasighat
articles.full_content:
The Press Information Bureau (PIB), Itanagar, successfully organised a Varta—a Media Outreach Programme (MOP)—on the theme “Role of Media in Times of Artificial Intelligence” at Pasighat today, in collaboration with the Pasighat Press Club.
Despite incessant rainfall, the programme witnessed enthusiastic participation from media persons across East Siang district, reflecting their keen interest in understanding the evolving interface between media and Artificial Intelligence. Their active engagement contributed to the overall success of the outreach initiative.
Breaking away from the confines of a conventional formal event, the programme adopted an interactive and engaging format, encouraging one-to-one discussions. The session was marked by lively exchanges, with journalists raising pertinent questions on the application, opportunities, and challenges posed by AI in media practices.
In his inaugural address, Shri Utsav Parmar, Joint Director, Central Bureau of Communication (CBC), Regional Office Itanagar, underscored the importance of balancing Artificial Intelligence with Natural Intelligence (NI). He highlighted that indigenous communities often possess a strong quotient of Natural Intelligence, which remains invaluable in contextual understanding. Emphasising the broader responsibilities of the media, he stated that the role of media extends beyond mere dissemination of news to ensuring clarity, accuracy, and context in information. He urged participants to harness AI as a tool, stating, “Make AI your own agent; do not become an agent of AI.”
The resource person for the programme, Shri Manish Awana, Assistant Professor at Rashtriya Raksha University, delivered an insightful session focusing on the factual reliability and inherent biases in AI systems. He pointed out that many AI models have limited data representation from the North Eastern region, which may lead to contextual inaccuracies and computational biases. He stressed the critical need for media professionals to verify and fact-check AI-generated content, cautioning that while AI enhances efficiency, it may sometimes compromise factual integrity if used uncritically.
The discussion also extended beyond AI, with participating journalists sharing their professional experiences and raising queries on various contemporary media-related issues. During the session, participants were introduced to the PB-SHABD platform, a valuable repository providing access to audio-visual content, which was well received by the attendees.
Special Guest of the MOP, Shri Maksam Tayeng, President of the Pasighat Press Club, in his address, earnestly urged the Press Information Bureau to continue organizing such meaningful outreach programmes in this region of Arunachal Pradesh, highlighting their vital role in strengthening media engagement and awareness.

Swachh Aadat se Swachh Bharat: Sustaining a National Effort
articles.full_content:
Cleanliness and sanitation are foundational to public health, dignity, and the quality of life. From the way waste is handled in neighbourhoods to how shared spaces are respected, these practices influence how communities function and grow. Over the years, the Swachh Bharat Mission has played a key role in bringing sanitation to the forefront of national priorities, strengthening systems , expanding toilet access, and encouraging public participation in waste management . This collective effort helped reframe sanitation from an individual matter to a shared civic responsibility.
While infrastructure enables change, it is everyday behaviour that sustains it. Long-term progress depends on habits that are practised consistently. Swachh Aadat se Swachh Bharat reflects this shift. Refusing single-use plastic, avoiding littering and spitting, practising waste segregation through green for wet waste and blue for dry waste, maintaining hand hygiene, using toilets responsibly, and reducing, reusing, recycling wherever possible are actions that, when repeated daily, shape cleaner surroundings.
Together, these habits form a practical framework for sustained cleanliness. They demonstrate that a cleaner India is built not through one-time efforts, but through steady participation, where responsible behaviour becomes part of everyday life, and progress continues through collective practice.
Everyday Actions, Lasting Change
Beyond policy and planning, the real impact of sanitation efforts is reflected in local action. From residential streets to city spaces, these examples show how regular engagement and ownership can steadily improve shared environments.
Transforming Waste into Public Art
At the MCD South Zonal Office in Delhi, discarded materials have found a new purpose through a creative waste-to-art initiative. Old pipes from condemned children’s play equipment and wheels salvaged from unusable dustbins were repurposed to create an installation that now welcomes visitors to the office premises.
Unveiled on Republic Day 2026, the initiative demonstrates how materials typically treated as waste can be reintegrated into public spaces through thoughtful reuse. By turning disposal into design, the project highlights the value of extending the life of materials while also creating visual engagement in civic spaces.
The installation reflects how resource-conscious choices, when integrated into routine planning, can support broader waste reduction efforts. It stands as an example of how Swachh Aadat se Swachh Bharat can take form through everyday decisions that prioritise reuse, responsibility and innovation.
Showcasing Swachh Practices in Uttar Pradesh
In Uttar Pradesh , the Swachh Bharat Mission tableau at the Parade Ground highlighted how everyday sanitation practices are being strengthened through integrated systems and citizen participation. Bhilai Steel Plant Revives School Safety Quiz After Two Decades; Nearly 500 Students Participate
articles.full_content:
In a significant step towards strengthening safety awareness among school students, the Safety Engineering Department of Steel Authority of India Limited–Bhilai Steel Plant (SAIL–BSP) has revived its school-level Safety Quiz Competition after a gap of more than two decades, engaging nearly 500 students of Classes IX and X.
The programme was organised at the BSP Senior Secondary School, Sector-10, with senior officials and educators highlighting the importance of inculcating safety consciousness from an early age. Shri Debdatta Satpathy, Chief General Manager (Safety and Fire Services), Shri S. K. Agrawal, General Manager In-Charge (Safety Engineering Department), and Smt. Shikha Dubey, General Manager (Education), attended as chief guests, while Smt. Sumita Sarkar, Principal of the school, was present as special guest.
The quiz competition witnessed enthusiastic participation from students and covered a wide range of themes, including fire safety, road safety, workplace safety, ergonomics, electrical safety, and environmental awareness. The programme commenced with a traditional lamp-lighting ceremony, followed by a welcome song presented by students along with faculty members.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Debdatta Satpathy expressed satisfaction over the revival of the initiative after more than twenty years and emphasised that safety extends beyond industrial environments to homes, roads, and playgrounds. He urged students to imbibe safety as a core life value and evolve as responsible citizens and future leaders.
Shri S. K. Agrawal, in his address, underscored that safety should not be viewed merely as a set of rules but as a deeply ingrained habit. Highlighting key environments such as schools, roads, homes, and digital spaces, he elaborated on essential safety practices, including adherence to laboratory protocols, cautious use of electricity and fire, road discipline, and responsible behaviour on social media. He cautioned that even momentary negligence could have lifelong consequences.
Smt. Sumita Sarkar stressed that safety is often neglected until an incident occurs and called for consistent practice to build safe habits. She particularly highlighted the growing importance of digital safety and the risks associated with unknown online interactions, expressing confidence that such initiatives would enhance students’ awareness and critical thinking.
The event was coordinated by officials of the Safety Engineering Department, including Shri Ajay Talloo, Assistant General Manager, Shri Arijit Roy, Deputy Manager, Shri Akhil Mishra, Assistant Manager, and Ms. Laxmi Dewangan, Junior Executive Assistant. The programme was compered by Smt. R. Sicily Dinkar, Senior Lecturer, while the school faculty ensured smooth conduct under the leadership of the Principal.
